Period: Top-up to Your Own CPF (SA / MA / RA)
Ordinary (OA): Cash contributions you make to your own CPF Special, MediSave or Retirement Accounts
MediSave (MA): Must be cash top-up (not CPF transfer) and within allowable top-up limits (e.g., up to FRS/MA caps)

Period: Top-up to Family Members’ CPF (SA / RA / MA)
Ordinary (OA): Cash top-ups you make to eligible family members’ CPF accounts
MediSave (MA): Recipients must be qualified family members (parents, parents-in-law, grandparents, grandparents-in-law, spouse, siblings) and meet income/disability criteria

Ordinary (OA): Total relief when combining your own + family top-ups
MediSave (MA): Not automatic — each category capped separately (S$8k each)

Ordinary (OA): Maximum total allowable tax relief across all relief types including CPF top-ups
MediSave (MA): Applies to all reliefs you claim in a YA, including CPF

Ordinary (OA): No annual income cap for eligibility
≤ S$8,000 annual income in preceding year or disabled
Special (SA): Must meet income or disability condition to qualify relief

Ordinary (OA): N/A (self top-ups always eligible, subject to caps)
Special (SA): Must be cash top-up and within CPF limits
